description: Learn when to talk to kids about sex, death, drugs, divorce, and social media. Parent's guide with expert recommendations. Free calculator.
intro: Parenting and family well-being require thoughtful planning across multiple dimensions: health, finances, education, and quality time. Each stage—from pregnancy through infancy, childhood, and adolescence—has distinct milestones, costs, and important decisions. This guide helps you identify the right age to introduce difficult topics, with recommendations updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.

explanation:
**Family and parenting** involve health, finances, and education. Each developmental stage has distinct milestones, expenses, and important decisions.

## How the Calculator Works

This calculator helps you identify the recommended age to introduce difficult topics to your child, based on developmental psychology research and pediatric guidelines.

## Important Notes

This is guidance only. For critical decisions—medical, financial, or legal—consult a qualified professional. Recommendations are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What age should I talk to my child about sex?
faq.a: Start with proper names for body parts around age 3. Introduce more details progressively as they grow and ask questions.

faq.q: How do I explain death to a young child?
faq.a: Between ages 4-6, explain death honestly without evasion. Use clear language and answer their questions calmly.

faq.q: When should I talk to my child about drugs?
faq.a: Start age-appropriate prevention conversations around 8-10. By age 12+, be honest about risks and real-world impacts.

faq.q: How do I tell my child about divorce?
faq.a: Always be honest and reassuring. Make clear that the divorce is not the child's fault and both parents still love them.

faq.q: When should children have social media accounts?
faq.a: Discuss limits and expectations before they open accounts. Set clear boundaries about time, content, and privacy.
These are guidelines based on developmental psychology. Every child is different—adjust based on maturity and family values.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free and require no registration.
Based on internationally validated guidelines and research. However, these are recommendations only—consult a professional for specific concerns.

faq.q: Do you store my personal information?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't collect or send any data to external servers.
